chapters.title: Design thinking is a way to bring meaning into your work
chapters.text: The modern workplace is in a state of constant change. Companies keep shifting, getting more agile, and moving faster to meet new demands. In this shifting world, it is up to workers to define their own success and joy. With that, you can avoid staying in a job that does not bring you happiness. Management teams should work with their staff to find a fair middle ground, and they should offer resources to build the right culture at work. The culture should help workers feel at ease with the fast changes around them as the business adapts to new market needs. Since we spend most of our time at work, we look to our jobs to give us meaning and impact. A dream work life is the shared job of the company and each worker. As a worker, you can build a plan that highlights the meaning and impact of what you do. There is no point in waiting for change to show up on its own. You have to act to make it happen. In this summary, you will learn more about designing your life in a way that brings you joy. How can you turn work into a place of strength and joy. Why is it sometimes smart to set the bar low for your role and the people around you. You will find the answers here. You will also learn that quitting is not always a bad move, and not something to avoid by default. Following this advice, you will become happier, healthier, and more productive. Turn the page to learn more.

chapters.title: Do not wait for happiness, work toward what you want
chapters.text: Waiting for a happy place to show up on its own is a waste of energy. A better paying job does not always lift our lives. The point is whether we know how to value what we have now and where we are headed. If the work matches our values and fits a stage of our growth, it will bring satisfaction. But how can we know if a given activity is right for us. We should pay attention to our feelings. When we feel anger, irritation, and despair, that is a signal that we are not meeting our needs. To change it, we have to act. Do not worry if you are not where you want to be. The job you have now is a stepping stone. That does not mean you should settle for a job that does not fulfill you, but the grass is not always greener. Of course, this way of thinking does not apply when work leaves you drained and is the cause of depression. But knowing that what you do is okay for now is a useful way to reframe the situation. You take the first step toward change by letting go of useless complaints and accepting the facts. Yes, you may not have your dream job yet, but if you can work and earn, that is good. Now you can think about how to make things better. What does it mean to reframe. It means labeling something in a new way. We should know the gap between a healthy reframe and a harmful one. Simply saying that your job is great, engaging, and well paid is not enough. Real change is needed to improve the situation. It may sound like a huge task, but the effort can be small. Small steps matter a lot. When we look for ways to make work more enjoyable, we are redesigning. To see this better, imagine you are fixing up your home. First, you walk around and check the state. Next, you picture what you want. Repaint the walls, swap the furniture, or even change the layout. Then you bring the plan to life. The same applies to redesigning life situations. Before you chase new ones, see if you can lift the ones you have.

chapters.title: Make the most of a bad spot by trying to change it
chapters.text: You probably know the saying, when life gives you lemons, make lemonade. Sometimes turning a bad spot into a good one works. For example, a vacation can ease the problem for a while if you feel worn out at work. But if all you do is make the best of a bad spot, it is still bad. The deep problem has not moved. You will feel better for a bit, but it is like patching a leaky pipe that will burst again later. Reframing means looking at the situation with care and setting aside strong feelings and exaggerations. Let us say you are thinking of leaving your job because you do not feel valued. You are flooded with feelings, and your first urge is to quit. Give yourself time for the feelings to settle. Then ask, what is happening with me right now, and what do I need. These questions help you see the situation from another side and find a fix. Do not wait. Ask your boss for feedback on your work. You can even ask for that feedback on a regular basis. You will reshape your work life by using a new way of thinking. It may not be fast or simple, but it will pay off and you can do it. Another method is the Set the Bar Low method, which says we need to start with small steps to reach bigger goals. You cannot write a book in one night. Writing 200 to 400 words a day is doable. To find joy in what you do, set goals you can hit, do them, and repeat. The mindset of always chasing more often leaves us feeling unfulfilled and down. When we chase the perfect job, car, house, or city, we never stop. Sometimes it helps to see the value in what you already have. Long term memory works best between 6 and 7 in the morning, and the brain soaks up information from that time with little effort.

chapters.title: Do not use the wrong compass for work and life
chapters.text: Do you put money over meaning in life. Many people wrestle with that question. So what do you pick. There is no single right answer, since it is a false split. Money and meaning are not enemies. They are different ways of measuring what you value. Many people have fancy jobs and still feel empty. A prestigious title does not guarantee meaning. At the same time, you can find a lot of meaning in work that pays very little. You can also have both money and meaning in the same work. Whichever you lean toward between money and meaning is not the point. What matters is a coherent life, where your values and your actions line up to make you happy. We often live someone else life without seeing it. We work around the clock because our parents told us that only people with a successful career win respect. Ask yourself if you agree with that view. If not, you are unlikely to feel at peace. Start small, set the bar low, and try something, as Bill Burnett has said. Your life is coherent when your identity, beliefs, and actions agree. To build a coherent life, you need to form clear views of work and life. The work view is your idea of what makes a job worth doing or not. When you build a work view, it is key to know why you work, what your work means, and how you affect others. A life view is your own take on what gives life purpose and value. A life view is what helps us see what matters most. It answers the big questions. We want to know the purpose of our lives. To find our life compass, we need to look at where we are and name the point we want to reach. A 2015 Lancet study found that people who work more than 55 hours a week face a higher risk of stroke, about 1.3 times the risk of those who stick to a standard week.

chapters.title: Build a healthy mindset to feel engaged and whole
chapters.text: When we feel stuck in a rut, we often look to blame other things or people around us. We do not need to point fingers to move forward. Let us face the truth. The people around us do not decide our happiness. We are not hostages to the situation. It is our choice to be where we are. If we do not like the company we work for or our boss, we are the only ones who can do something about it. Life design means taking ownership of your choices. You are the one who can start making positive changes to move toward joy on every level. There are two types of mindset, based on research by Stanford professor Carol Dweck. They are the fixed mind and the growth mind. People with a fixed mind believe that each of us has a fixed set of natural talents that we cannot change. They think they can only become an artist if they were born with that gift. When they are good at something, they chalk it up to natural talent, not to practice and growth. People with a growth mind believe that natural talent is not the only thing that decides success. Everyone has a starting point, but we can all grow and reach new heights. We only need to work on our skills in a steady way. They credit success to hard work and discipline, not to natural gifts. People with a fixed mind often have a harder time with failure and do not try to change the situation. They push the cause of the loss onto outside factors. The good news is that no matter where you start, you can shift. If you have a fixed mind now, you can retune it to a growth mind with practice and training. Whenever you catch yourself thinking in a fixed way, look at the situation from another angle and suggest what you can do to lift it.

chapters.title: Do not quit your job, redesign it
chapters.text: Sometimes you are bored with your job and fed up. Maybe you started a career and never planned to stay, but many years have passed and you are still there. Do not quit. Not yet. You do not have to stay in this job forever. Nor should you hold on at all costs. But people often act on impulse and make fast calls, missing the chance to make things better in the same spot. Sometimes staying is more effective than starting from scratch. Redesigning our lives can be hard, and starting over from zero can be even harder. It is better to try all your redesign moves before you opt for a full reset. Life design is about building many options and prototypes for the life you want. There are several ways to change a situation that does not fit you. Depending on the setting, one of these will work. The first path is relabel and re enlist. Try to find a fresh spark in your work. Recall what drew you to it in the first place. Feel free to share new ideas and solutions. Figure out what you need to feel valued and give it to yourself. Sometimes small things, like a more comfortable desk or steady feedback from your boss, can lift the situation. Reframing and re enlisting are good ways to make things good enough for now. It is pretty simple. Take things as they are. Find new reasons for what you do. Redefine your bond with work. Refresh your interest in the role. Find reasons to stay motivated on the job. The second path is redesign. Why not make a structural or even a cosmetic change to the job you already have. That way you are rebuilding and refreshing it. For example, moving to another department or trying a new direction in the same company is a structural change. It takes extra effort, but it can be the fastest way out of a tough spot. Life design is a journey, so let go of the end goal and focus on the process and see what comes next, as Bill Burnett has said.

chapters.title: Reframe quitting as a choice and leave with purpose
chapters.text: Nothing in life stays the same forever, and everything has to change at some point. You are unlikely to work for the same company your whole life. Change and newness are needed for growth, so learning to quit the right way is key. Quitting can happen in many ways, but they all fall into two camps. The first type burns bridges. It usually shows someone storming out in a flash, without a care. No matter how bad things get, you should not give in to your base instincts. Walk out the door with your dignity and the relationship intact. Anger and irritation have no place when you tell a company you are leaving. The second type gives two weeks notice. It is used thousands of times a day and often looks like this. The person decides they want to quit. They pick the date they will leave. They tell the company in a respectful way, two weeks before the date. This way of leaving is correct, but it can still be better. If you are in a setting you cannot control, this is fine. It is not as harmful as the first way, but it is not helpful either. Then there is generative quitting. Reframing is useful not only in work changes but also in the process of changing jobs. It all depends on how you read the situation. You can see the need to leave as a bad thing or as an open door for new chances. The end of the old always means the start of the new. You have gained experience you can now use elsewhere. See a layoff as a chance to grow. To step into the next stage with joy, we need to close the previous one with thanks. You surely have reasons to be grateful for the time you spent. You just need to notice them. People who quit in a generative way know how important it is to leave the employer on good terms. As a result, they limit the damage and end up with a far better outcome.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text: Designing a life is always a journey with no finish line. The unknown of what comes next can be unsettling, yet it is also what makes life feel alive. Of course, sometimes our plans fall apart, and we need to pause, take a breath, and take stock. That often means starting again, and that is fine. Once you adopt design thinking, you will keep moving forward, because you will always have at least a rough sense of what is next. Your wins may even top your hopes. Building a life is a process that never ends, and it is never polished, but it can be good enough. We know that life is short and far too short to be spent dreading the workday. Each of us deserves to feel at peace with the hours we give to our work. Take a moment to ask the following. Do you have to drag yourself to the office. Do you feel a sting of irritation when you deal with coworkers and managers. Are you content with the output you produce. Do you feel tense and worn out once a workday ends. If most of your answers lean yes, burnout may be near. Now is the time to look after yourself. Try this. Write out your work view and your life view. What shifts do you need to make in your days and your job so what you do lines up with the values you hold. Keep a journal and scan for patterns in any body signs you notice, like a sore back or pounding head. It may be a quiet signal of burnout. Try to keep a positive mindset. Reframe any dark thought that pops up. If you have read to the end, well done.
